name: Truth in Lending Act (TILA) / Regulation Z
summary: Federal consumer credit disclosure law requiring standardized APR and fee disclosures.

evidence.claim: TILA (15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq., implemented by CFPB Regulation Z, 12 CFR Part 1026) requires lenders to disclose APR, finance charge, amount financed, and total payments in a standardized format.
CFPB 2024 interpretive rule treats Pay-in-4 BNPL providers as 'credit card' issuers under TILA, subjecting them to disclosure obligations and chargeback rights.

body.officialName: Truth in Lending Act / Regulation Z
body.scopeSummary: Requires standardized disclosure of APR, finance charge, amount financed, total of payments, and payment schedule in a Schumer-Box-like format for consumer credit. Also grants right of rescission on certain home-secured loans. CFPB enforces (E1). BNPL 'pay-in-4' lenders are now covered as credit-card issuers (E2).
body.userRightsPlainLanguage: Any lender extending credit to US consumers must give you the APR, total cost, and payment schedule in a standardized box BEFORE you sign. For refinanced home loans, you have 3 business days to rescind. Buy-Now-Pay-Later providers must also provide these disclosures and honor chargebacks like credit card issuers.

enforcementSignals.description: CFPB is the primary TILA enforcer.
